live_render_blocker: glbopt-stage mutation passes mba.verify() but is rejected at ctree with INTERR 50346; clean live render needs mutating in the optblock/GLBOPT1 pass (d810 BlockOptimizerManager) where IDA re-optimizes the inserts
root_cause_verify_cpp: 50346 not in SDK verify.cpp/cverify.cpp (raised deeper in hexx64); verify.cpp:1829 documents the sibling-class 50409 = microcode modified without informing the decompiler (returning 0 from a callback).
interr_50346_reversed: hexx64 sub_18013C4F0 (post-global-opt finalizer; INTERR site 0x18013d7e3, Win base). Predicate: if (mba->flags & MBA_LVARS0 /*0x40000*/) { v4 = *(int*)(*(mba+0x310)+0x30); if (v4 & 1) INTERR(50346); if (v4 & 2) INTERR(50347); } then mba->flags |= MBA_GLBOPT|MBA_CHVARS (0x210000). mba+0x310 is a PRIVATE member beyond the public mba_t (size 0x310). So 50346 is a STATE-CONSISTENCY assertion on a private mba dirty-bit gated by MBA_LVARS0 -- NOT a CFG-shape check. No cheap edge-tweak fix exists; confirms the optblock-stage mutation path (where the decompiler re-runs and keeps this state consistent). IDB comment persisted at 0x18013d7e3.
mbl_keep_note: insert_nop_blk sets MBL_KEEP via copy_block_keep, but the inserts are reachable so KEEP is incidental; verify is clean regardless
state_write_cleanup_note: mba.verify() is clean without queue_zero_state_write; the render gap is the mutation STAGE, not state-write residue
